35f62abd-9802-808b-a9b9-fb8ce9e931bd:
# Adaptive Scheduler

This is my project **Adaptive Scheduler** — a voice-first reminder app built with the **Groq API**, going beyond "set a timer" to actually reason about your schedule.

## Highlights

- **Pre-alerts** — preps u before something like an exam, not just pinging on time
- **Follow-up nudges** — checks in after a task's duration to see if u actually did it
- **Converse with the agent** — ask *"do i have a gap today"* and it computes real free time from ur schedule, not a guess
- **Voice edit & delete** — just say the reminder's title to edit or delete it

Built with a router + 4 separate prompts (create/update/delete/query) instead of one giant prompt, and a stateless backend that rebuilds context from the full conversation history each time — no sessions to juggle.
38d62abd-9802-8015-9d77-d3168b421580:
# Maze2Race 🏁

A pathfinding visualizer comparing **BFS**, **Dijkstra**, and **A\*** on a maze board — a project a lot of people build, but with a few things I wanted to add myself.

## Highlights

- **Three boards** — an open field to draw ur own walls, a generated maze with loops, and an unreachable one to test that the algorithms correctly say "no path found"
- **Slo-mo mode** — watch the algorithm explore node by node, with speed control, pause and stop
- **Compare mode** — run all three algorithms side by side on the same board and see nodes covered + path cost at a glance
- **Draggable start/end** — move them anywhere and re-run instantly

**A\*** ended up exploring way fewer nodes than Dijkstra by combining real cost-so-far with a heuristic guess to the destination — same shortest path, less wasted exploration.
